subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, WJP, RSF) + South African official (Stats SA, SARB, Eskom, SAPS, Treasury) + South African independent press (Daily Maverick, News24, Bhekisisa, GroundUp).
Strengths cluster in institutions: a credible independent central bank, an assertive judiciary, respected statistics, and a free press; weaknesses cluster in delivery and security.

Standout drags: a deteriorating fiscal path, very high violent crime, extreme inequality and unemployment, and a hollowed civil service. Source-lean: HIGH but low-distortion Anglophone (domestic discourse is in English).

economic.notes: A credible central bank and sound, sophisticated banks, against a deteriorating fiscal path and low growth.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~77% GDP rising (IMF projects ~81% by 2028), persistent deficits, SOE/Eskom contingent liabilities, and only ~1.3-1.8% growth. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The SARB is genuinely credible and independent (it adopted a lower 3% target and is willing to hike) - a real strength. (SARB; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The rand is a free-floating non-reserve currency, notably volatile. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Averaging ~3.2% within the band, well-managed. (IMF; SARB)

banking_stability.notes: A sophisticated, well-regulated, sound banking sector with deep capital markets - a durable strength. (IMF FSAP)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Mining (gold/platinum/coal) plus autos with commodity-cycle exposure and acute US-trade/AGOA risk, partly offset by institutional diversification. (ISS Africa; tralac)

institutional.notes: An independent judiciary, respected statistics, and a free press, dragged by a state-capture-hollowed civil service.

rule_of_law.notes: An independent, assertive judiciary (state-capture accountability) is a genuine strength, dragged by weak prosecution and enforcement. (WJP 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Stats SA is credible, transparent, and internationally respected. (Stats SA; IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Hollowed by state capture, with weak service delivery and municipal/SOE dysfunction. (IMF; OECD)

press_freedom.notes: A free, robust independent press; RSF rates it 'satisfactory.' (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Strong constitutional protections, against extreme inequality, unemployment, and coalition fragility.

political_stability.notes: A 2024 unity-government coalition (the long-dominant party lost its majority) that functions but is tense, having survived budget standoffs; 2026 local elections a test. (News24; tralac)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong constitutional protections, an active Constitutional Court, and robust civil society. (V-Dem; WJP)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful retirement and critical-skills visa routes exist but are bureaucratic and slow, against persistent xenophobia toward African migrants. (GroundUp; News24)

social_cohesion.notes: Among the world's highest inequality (a Gini ~0.63) and ~31-33% unemployment (youth ~46%+), with racial and economic cleavages. (Stats SA; World Bank)

demographics.notes: A relatively young population, weighed by a heavy HIV legacy and disease burden. (Stats SA; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A non-aligned BRICS member with no external war, but significant 2025 friction with the US.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned and a BRICS member, with significant 2025 US friction (an aid halt, a 30% tariff, an expropriation row) - unpredictable Western reliability. (CNN; SA press)

conflict_involvement.notes: No external war; the main risk is internal unrest (the 2021 riots a precedent). (ISS Africa)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An exchange-control legacy, substantially liberalised, with modernisation ongoing. (SARB; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: A functional insurance market, against very high violent crime, two-tier healthcare, and fragile infrastructure.

climate.notes: A drought/water crisis (the Cape Town 'Day Zero' legacy) and floods, with a coal-heavy, contested energy transition. (World Bank)

healthcare.notes: Deeply two-tier (a strong private sector versus a strained public one), with a contested NHI rollout and a high disease burden. (Bhekisisa; SAMJ)

infrastructure.notes: Load-shedding much improved in 2025 but fragile, with persistent rail/port/water decay. (Eskom; IMF)

crime_safety.notes: Very high violent crime, with a homicide rate ~45/100k. (SAPS; Daily Maverick)

disaster_insurance.notes: A functional, sophisticated insurance market against meaningful drought/flood exposure. (industry)

summary: A country of strong institutions and weak delivery: a genuinely credible independent central bank, an assertive judiciary that pursued state-capture accountability, respected statistics, a free press, sophisticated banks, and strong constitutional protections - set against a deteriorating fiscal path, a state-capture-hollowed civil service, very high violent crime, among the world's highest inequality and unemployment, two-tier healthcare, and fragile (if improved) electricity and logistics. The 2024 unity-government coalition is a functioning but tense democratic test, and 2025 brought significant friction with the US.
